Title 9 — Public Peace, Morals & Welfare Plain-Language Service Free · no account

A plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 9 (Public Peace, Morals & Welfare) — the County's public-conduct ordinances, including trespassing (Ch. 9.04) and the minors' curfew (Ch. 9.24). This title is mostly prohibitions enforced by the Police, not a permit service. Structural first version; sections marked expanding are being sourced verbatim; nothing here is legal advice.

1 · What Title 9 covers sourced

A structural overview of the public-conduct title. We name only what is in the Code's own structure.

Title 9 (Public Peace, Morals & Welfare, §§9.04.010–9.36.050) is the County's public-conduct title. It includes Chapter 9.04 (Trespassing) and Chapter 9.24 (Curfew) — which makes it unlawful for minors to be in public places during specified late-night hours — among other public-order provisions. Unlike permit titles, Title 9 is enforced directly by the Police Department and the courts; there's nothing to apply for. Chapter-level detail is expanding as the corpus is sourced.

Maui County Code Title 9; Ch. 9.04 (Trespassing), Ch. 9.24 (Curfew). full chapter list + each offense's elements

2 · Key chapters sourced

The provisions residents ask about most. Always read the chapter for the exact rule.

ChapterSubjectIn plain terms
9.04TrespassingEntering or staying on property without right or permission
9.24Curfew (minors)Minors not in public places during specified late-night hours
othersPublic peace & welfareadditional public-order offenses — expanding

Maui County Code Title 9 (§§9.04–9.36). complete chapter inventory

3 · Enforcement sourced

How Title 9 is enforced. Not legal advice.

Title 9 offenses are enforced by the Police Department and prosecuted through the courts, layered over State criminal law. Note: the zoning-enforcement chapter §19.530.030 reaches MCC titles 8, 12, 14, 16, 18, 19 and 20 — it does not list Title 9, and the §15-2/MC-15 fine schedule is specific to Titles 16·18·19. So Title 9 runs on its own penalty provisions and State law, not the civil code-enforcement engine.

Maui County Code Title 9 + State criminal law. Specific penalties expanding.

4 · The lens — order vs. liberty analysis

Labeled analysis — public-conduct law read through two charters. Not adopted law; an interpretive lens beside the Code.

Maui County Charter — lensUnder the Maui County Charter, the Council may legislate for public peace, health, and welfare, and the Police enforce it. The Charter frames these ordinances as protecting the community's shared order and safety.
Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) — lensUnder the Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) lens, every person carries inherent dignity and freedom of movement; public-order rules are legitimate only where they protect others from real harm. The lens reads measures like a curfew as a narrow, last-resort tool, and insists enforcement be even-handed and non-discriminatory.
⚖️ Position — analysis, not lawProtect the peace, but keep public-conduct law narrow, clear, and evenly enforced — order in service of liberty, not the other way around. Same title, read two ways.
Free now (no account): this whole page is Tier 1 — free. No secret keys (environment references only). This service is informational and does not provide legal advice.
